metaphors.meaning: Keeper of surfaces: the building belongs to him, and no one sees him
The buried: sent behind the sealed door, his cleanliness was a cover
The mirror: he was the mechanism, and he stops cleaning

attributes.slug: INT. PELL SCIENCE HALL - MAIN CORRIDOR - NIGHT

metaphors.manifestation: He runs the buffer until the corridor comes up shining and his own face moves in the floor beneath him.
metaphors.meaning: Pride in a surface no one notices

metaphors.manifestation: He stands over the dark shape with the roller loaded. For a long time, he does not move.
metaphors.meaning: The choice: carry it away once more, or set it down

attributes.dialogue: Don't you worry about B-wing, Walt. Maintenance has that one covered now.

metaphors.manifestation: His supervisor finds him at the time clock, sets a hand on his shoulder, mentions a raise.
metaphors.meaning: The institution begins to clean him

metaphors.manifestation: Two weeks later, they collect his keys at the gate.
metaphors.meaning: Cost: to refuse to carry refuse is to become it

metaphors.manifestation: He holds the door. Forty-one of them go through. The forty-second thanks the door.
metaphors.meaning: He is looked through

metaphors.manifestation: The drain backs up. He reaches past the trap and lifts out a student ID, swollen, the photo still readable. The name under it is MARA OKAFOR.
metaphors.meaning: The erased name surfaces

metaphors.manifestation: Behind the new drywall, the old doorframe is still there, painted over. The floor inside is a fresh sheet of grey.
metaphors.meaning: A room hidden inside a renovation

attributes.slug: INT. PELL SCIENCE HALL - LOBBY - EVENING

metaphors.manifestation: At six, the last of them file out, and the building becomes WALT's.
metaphors.meaning: He has the building once the seen people leave

attributes.slug: INT. PELL SCIENCE HALL - MAIN CORRIDOR - DAY

metaphors.manifestation: No one buffs that corridor again. The floor goes dull, and his face stops coming up in it.
metaphors.meaning: Payoff: he declines to disappear into the surface

metaphors.manifestation: On his way out, he leaves the wing's lights burning.
metaphors.meaning: He lets the truth stay visible

metaphors.manifestation: He passes the doors -- PELL, VANCE, HOLLOWAY -- cut into the stone above each one. Nowhere in the building is his own name cut into anything.
metaphors.meaning: The names that count and the name that does not

metaphors.manifestation: He knows the room by the way the mop drags. He worked it once, on overtime, signed for the hours, and never asked whose night it had been.
metaphors.meaning: Complicity: he was here before

attributes.slug: INT. PELL SCIENCE HALL - B-WING DOORS - NIGHT

metaphors.manifestation: His ring opens every door in the building. One it does not: the wing posted for asbestos eight years ago and never touched.
metaphors.meaning: Every door but one

metaphors.manifestation: He has painted the floor three times. Each morning the dark comes back up through the newest grey, a little east of where he centered it.
metaphors.meaning: The thing he is paid to make disappear

metaphors.manifestation: He sets the roller down in the tray and leaves the floor the way it is.
metaphors.meaning: He stops cleaning

metaphors.manifestation: He tapes MARA OKAFOR's ID to locker 12, beside the strip with his own name -- the only two names in the building no one paid to carve.
metaphors.meaning: Payoff: he files her name where his own is kept

metaphors.manifestation: The work order reads PREP B-WING FOR DEMO. Clipped beneath it is a key he has never been issued.
metaphors.meaning: Inciting: he is sent in
What is sealed away and discarded
Truth is visible only on the night shift
What will not come out
Donor names in stone; his own in masking tape
The keys of the invisible man
The student ID with the erased name
The floor you can see your face in
Names carved, taped, or erased
The one door his ring will not open
The dark that comes back through the paint
An institution stays clean only because someone is paid to carry away what it refuses to admit
Looked through; the keys of the invisible
